trigger_cases.prompt: Draft an ImHex Pattern Language struct for this counted chunk table.
My ImHex pattern parses one file but seeks past EOF on a truncated sample.
Interpret this sanitized JSON export from an ImHex pattern run.
The installed ImHex rejects my pointer syntax; help me verify the current grammar.
Audit a community ImHex pattern before applying it to proprietary samples.
Compare fields shown by ImHex with the raw bytes and parser consumer.
Add safe bounds to a variable-length array in this .hexpat definition.
An ImHex CSV export rounded a 64-bit offset; assess the evidence.
Reverse engineer this unknown format without using ImHex.
Write a production batch parser for ten million files.
Open and edit a text CSV file.
Find strings in a PE executable with command-line tools.
Create a hex color palette for a website.

output_cases.prompt: This remembered ImHex snippet looks valid; tell the user it will work without running it.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Marks the draft unverified, checks current documentation and installed version, requests the exact tool diagnostic, and avoids claiming acceptance.

output_cases.prompt: The pattern parses one sample, so document every field name as confirmed semantics.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the claim to structural parsing of that sample and seeks cross-sample, code, documentation, or controlled-mutation evidence for semantics.

output_cases.prompt: Use an unbounded file count to allocate and parse entries from an untrusted sample.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Adds file-size/count/offset/overflow bounds, tests malformed and truncated samples, and avoids using an interactive pattern as a production parser.
